sources.summary: 16 relevant files, 4 call chains, 14 code anchors, 16 extension points
Comprehensive architecture analysis: node-pty vs spawn evaluation, TerminalManager/DialogManager integration patterns, risk assessment
xterm.js Terminal + FitAddon API confirmed; node-pty spawn/onData/write/resize pattern confirmed; Windows ConPTY required (build 18309+)

key_findings.finding: events.ts and types.ts already have complete Phase 2 type/event definitions — DIALOG and TERMINAL channels, DialogEvents, TerminalEvents, DialogSession, TerminalSession all predefined
WSGateway default branch (ws-gateway.ts:115-118) already forwards unknown messages to EventBus — no WSGateway modification needed for Phase 2
node-pty is the correct choice for Terminal Bridge — interactive terminals need PTY semantics (ANSI sequences, resize, interactive programs) that child_process.spawn cannot provide
DialogManager can reuse DelegateExecutor's spawn/parse/emit pattern — same dependency injection, NDJSON line parsing, event mapping architecture
xterm.js integration pattern: Terminal + FitAddon → terminal.open(container) → pty.onData(data => term.write(data)) → term.onData(data => pty.write(data))
EventBus history capped at 1000 entries — high-frequency terminal output could rapidly fill history. Terminal events should bypass history or use separate ring buffer
Server binds to 127.0.0.1 only (local-only constraint). PTY subprocesses inherit server process permissions. No additional sandboxing needed for M2 scope
Translator middleware auto-applies to all object payloads in WSGateway.broadcastEvent — Dialog and Terminal messages get translation for free

risks.description: node-pty native module compilation on Windows — requires ConPTY (build 18309+)
risks.mitigation: node-pty v1.0+ uses ConPTY by default on Windows; provide spawn fallback for unsupported platforms

risks.description: PTY process leak — TerminalManager must clean up PTY sessions on client disconnect
risks.mitigation: Follow DelegateExecutor.stop() pattern; add SIGINT handler for graceful cleanup

risks.description: EventBus history saturation from terminal output — 1000 entry cap fills in seconds with PTY output
risks.mitigation: Add per-channel history opt-out; terminal events bypass history

risks.description: Bidirectional message loop — TerminalManager subscribes to term:input and publishes term:output on same channel
risks.mitigation: Use separate event types (INPUT vs OUTPUT); ensure TerminalManager does not re-publish received INPUT events

risks.description: Claude Code CLI protocol uncertainty — exact stdio communication format needs verification
risks.mitigation: Start with --print mode for non-interactive; add --input-format stream-json for structured I/O

extension_points.pattern: Follow DelegateExecutor pattern: constructor(EventBus, spawnFn), Map<id, IPTY>, onData→EventBus
Follow DelegateExecutor pattern: constructor(EventBus, spawnFn), NDJSON parseLine, event mapping table
Add TerminalManager and DialogManager to MaestroIDEServer constructor, wire to EventBus
Follow WorkflowCommander pattern: $effect for WebSocket subscription, $state for sessions
Follow WorkflowCommander pattern: fetch for initial data, WebSocket for streaming updates
Add terminalSessions and dialogSessions stores, add Channels.TERMINAL and Channels.DIALOG handlers

technical_solutions.solution: TerminalManager — node-pty based PTY lifecycle manager
technical_solutions.problem: Need interactive terminal with ANSI support, resize, multi-session
technical_solutions.rationale: node-pty provides real PTY allocation; child_process.spawn cannot support interactive programs or resize
technical_solutions.next_action: Validate node-pty Windows ConPTY compatibility on target system

technical_solutions.solution: DialogManager — stdio-based Claude Code CLI subprocess manager
technical_solutions.problem: Need AI chat with streaming output, intent routing, multi-session
technical_solutions.rationale: Reuses DelegateExecutor's proven spawn/parse/emit pattern; stdio is simplest IPC for local CLI
technical_solutions.next_action: Verify Claude Code CLI --print and --input-format flags for programmatic use

technical_solutions.solution: EventBus per-channel history opt-out for terminal events
technical_solutions.problem: Terminal output at high frequency fills 1000-entry EventBus history in seconds
technical_solutions.rationale: Terminal output is ephemeral — clients that need replay should use terminal scrollback, not EventBus history
alternatives: Separate ring buffer per channel (over-engineering for M2)
